title: Ctenopteris alfaroi
intro: Ctenopteris alfaroi, also known as C. alfaroi, is a plant species within the Ctenopteris genus of ferns, specifically a type of climbing fern native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ctenopteris alfaroi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Ctenopteris alfaroi is used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Ctenopteris alfaroi is classified within the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the lily-pileum or bracken family. This family is part of the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The order Polypodiales is further divided into several suborders, with Ctenopteris alfaroi falling under the suborder Polypodioideae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other species within the Pteridaceae family. The family Pteridaceae is a diverse group of ferns, with over 3,000 species worldwide.
